title: Mandatory Returns and Unroutable Publication · Program Defense
summary: A release board compares near-miss Go and RabbitMQ decisions for mandatory returns and unroutable publication and accepts only bounded ownership, causal evidence, and an explicit live-broker transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a correlated Go return and route-evidence ledger. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Register and drain return notifications before mandatory publication and correlate content, exchange, routing key, properties, and publisher intent.
Distinguish broker confirms from consumer processing and combine mandatory returns or alternate routing with confirm results; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Build topology fixtures that assert expected destination sets, forbidden routes, unroutable cases, permissions, and changed bindings; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Mandatory Return Sequence · changed case · Quiz Mandatory Return Sequence
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go messaging team handles case 1766 during confirm-window load test with broker flow control. Build deterministic Go evidence to keep return, alternate custody, confirmation, and unknown route evidence separate; bound payload, queue, confirm-ledger, and handler bytes; then raise a broker resource alarm and halt new admission. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Register and drain return notifications before mandatory publication and correlate content, exchange, routing key, properties, and publisher intent.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Alternate Return Tradeoff
steps.instruction:

Build deterministic Go evidence to keep return, alternate custody, confirmation, and unknown route evidence separate; name the owner and stop path for every goroutine and notification channel; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose mandatory returns, alternate exchanges, or both from publisher feedback, broker custody, operational ownership, and observability needs; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

Evidence target: RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Alternate Return Tradeoff.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Alternate Return Tradeoff, keep Alternate Return Tradeoff connected to executable Go messaging decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Choose mandatory returns, alternate exchanges, or both from publisher feedback, broker custody, operational ownership, and observability needs; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

hints: For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Alternate Return Tradeoff, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Alternate Return Tradeoff precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Alternate Return Tradeoff, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A quiz Go messaging team handles case 7337 during outbox relay recovery after a database commit. Build deterministic Go evidence to keep return, alternate custody, confirmation, and unknown route evidence separate; name the owner and stop path for every goroutine and notification channel; then lose a quorum member and recompute majority availability. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Choose mandatory returns, alternate exchanges, or both from publisher feedback, broker custody, operational ownership, and observability needs; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

For RabbitMQ and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Alternate Return Tradeoff, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: Alternate exchanges provide the publisher the same immediate route-failure evidence as mandatory returns. In Go, publisher confirms and basic.return notifications have one guaranteed combined order.
